content: Maternal mortality reduction strategies: (1) Clinical interventions—active management of third stage of labor (oxytocin) preventing postpartum hemorrhage, magnesium sulfate for preeclampsia/eclampsia, antenatal corticosteroids for preterm birth, antibiotics for prelabor rupture of membranes; (2) Health system strengthening—skilled birth attendance, emergency obstetric care availability, blood transfusion services, referral systems, maternal death surveillance and response; (3) Policy approaches—Medicaid expansion improving insurance coverage, paid parental leave, doula/midwifery coverage, implicit bias training accountability; (4) Community interventions—community health workers, group prenatal care, home visiting programs, transportation assistance; (5) Postpartum care extension—continuous Medicaid coverage through 12 months postpartum, early postpartum visits, mental health screening; (6) Addressing disparities—community-based participatory approaches, diversifying workforce, addressing social needs. Maternal Mortality Review Committees identify prevention opportunities. Bundle approaches (AIM Initiative) standardize evidence-based practices.
